Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Represents a customer using the SharEat Customer Hub during a dining session. This entity stores basic customer identification for personalized greetings and session management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

name.description: The full name of the customer.

Table.title: Table
Table.description: Represents a dining table within the restaurant where a customer's session is active. This helps link customer requests to a specific physical location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Table entity.

tableNumber.description: The unique number or identifier assigned to the dining table.

section.description: The section of the restaurant where the table is located (e.g., 'Main Dining', 'Patio', 'Bar Area').

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Defines an item available on the restaurant's menu, specifically those eligible for 'Fast Refill Ordering'. It includes details pertinent to refill eligibility and cooldown periods.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

name.description: The name of the menu item (e.g., 'Coca-Cola', 'Iced Tea').

description.description: A brief description of the menu item.

category.description: The category the menu item belongs to (e.g., 'Beverages', 'Appetizers').

isRefillable.description: Indicates whether this menu item is available for a 'Fast Refill' request.

refillCooldownMinutes.description: The minimum number of minutes a customer must wait between refill requests for this specific item, to prevent abuse.

Session.title: Session
Session.description: Represents an active customer session, established after successful PIN authentication. It links a customer to a specific table and maintains session-specific details and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Session entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer associated with this session. (Relationship: Customer 1:1 Session)

tableId.description: Reference to the Table where this session is taking place. (Relationship: Table 1:1 Session)

startTime.description: The date and time when the customer session officially started.

endTime.description: The date and time when the session ended or was closed. Null if the session is still active.

status.description: The current operational status of the session (e.g., 'active', 'closed', 'pending_checkout').

welcomeMessage.description: A personalized welcome message displayed to the customer at the start of their session.

Request.title: Request
Request.description: Tracks all customer-initiated requests during a session, including 'Fast Refills', 'Call Server', and 'Request Add-ons'. Each request records its type, status, and associated details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Request entity.

sessionId.description: Reference to the Session during which this request was made. (Relationship: Session 1:N Request)

status.description: The current status of the request, indicating its progression (e.g., 'queued', 'accepted', 'preparing', 'served', 'rejected').

requestTime.description: The date and time when the customer initiated this request.

fulfillmentTime.description: The date and time when the request was ultimately fulfilled (served or rejected) by staff.

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem if the request type is 'refill'. Null for 'call_server' or 'add_on_inquiry' requests. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N Request)

quantity.description: The quantity of the MenuItem requested, applicable primarily for 'refill' requests.

notes.description: Any additional notes or specific instructions provided by the customer for this request.

definition.description: Globally accessible collection of menu items available for 'Fast Refill Ordering'. These documents are primarily read-only for customers. No authorization dependencies. Includes fields like `id`, `name`, `description`, `category`, `isRefillable`, `refillCooldownMinutes`.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: Unique identifier for a menu item.

definition.description: Globally accessible collection of dining tables. These documents are primarily read-only for customers. No authorization dependencies. Includes fields like `id`, `tableNumber`, `section`.

params.name: tableId
params.description: Unique identifier for a dining table.

definition.description: Backend-managed collection of customer profiles. This collection is not directly accessible by the authenticated customer's `request.auth.uid` in the SharEat Customer Hub. Customer-facing details like `name` are denormalized into `Session` documents for Authorization Independence.

params.name: customerId
params.description: Unique identifier for a customer profile.

definition.description: Stores active customer session details. This collection is central to customer authorization. It includes `userAuthUid` (the Firebase `uid` assigned to the authenticated user for this session, CRITICAL for authorization independence), `customerName` (denormalized from Customer for UI display), `tableNumber`, and `tableSection` (denormalized from Table for UI display). Accessible only by the `userAuthUid` tied to the session, ensuring Authorization Independence and QAP support.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Unique identifier for an active customer session.

definition.description: Stores individual customer-initiated requests made during a session. This subcollection relies on its parent session. It includes `userAuthUid` (denormalized from the parent Session's `userAuthUid`, CRITICAL for Authorization Independence and QAP support), `customerName` (denormalized from Session/Customer), `tableNumber` (denormalized from Session/Table), and `menuItemName` (if `menuItemId` is present, denormalized from MenuItem) for display purposes and to avoid `get()` calls in rules. Accessible only by the `userAuthUid` tied to the parent session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent customer session.

params.name: requestId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific customer request.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the provided CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES and DESIGN STRATEGY MANDATES. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & QAPs:**
To ensure Authorization Independence and enable secure Query As Authorization Predicates (QAPs), the primary authorization field, `userAuthUid` (representing `request.auth.uid`), is denormalized into both the `Session` and `Request` documents. When a customer successfully authenticates with a PIN, a temporary Firebase `uid` is assigned to their active session. This `userAuthUid` is stored directly within the `/sessions/{sessionId}` document. Crucially, for all subcollection documents (like `/sessions/{sessionId}/requests/{requestId}`), this `userAuthUid` is further denormalized into each `Request` document. This eliminates the need for `get()` operations in security rules to check parent session ownership, allowing atomic writes and simplifying rule logic to a direct comparison: `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userAuthUid`. This denormalization also directly supports QAPs for listing requests, as clients can query `db.collection('sessions/{sessionId}/requests').where('userAuthUid', '==', request.auth.uid)` with security rules safely enforcing this predicate.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
Collections are segregated based on their security posture. `/menu_items` and `/tables` are designed for public read access (to authenticated users generally, assuming staff might also read, but primarily for customer display). The `/customers` collection is intended for backend management and internal linking; customer-facing details like `name` and `tableNumber` are denormalized into the `Session` document to avoid dependencies for the customer UI. 

User-specific data follows a hierarchical path-based ownership model, starting with `/sessions/{sessionId}`. Each `Session` document directly contains the `userAuthUid` of the authenticated customer, making it a private document for that specific user. `Request` entities, which are 1:N to a `Session`, are stored as a subcollection `/sessions/{sessionId}/requests/{requestId}`. This maintains a clear logical hierarchy while the denormalized `userAuthUid` in each `Request` document ensures independent authorization.

**DBAC & Invariants:**
Roles are implicitly managed by the presence of `userAuthUid` in session-specific documents, signifying the user's role as an 'active session participant'. This avoids custom claims and relies solely on `request.auth.uid`. Invariants like ownership, timestamps (`startTime`, `requestTime`), and denormalized data (`customerName`, `tableNumber`, `menuItemName`) are explicitly maintained within the relevant documents, ensuring data integrity and reducing client-side joins.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
Explicit `status` fields are used in both `Session` and `Request` documents to model state clearly. Semantic naming conventions are strictly followed (e.g., `userAuthUid`, `customerId`, `tableId`, `{sessionId}`, `{requestId}`). Dynamic keys are avoided, and all authorization-relevant fields are consistent across documents.
